heaviest_rainfall.status: The most intense rainfall recorded took place on 09 November 2025, with precipitation totalling 47.89mm.  The rain fell at 24.6mm per hour at times over the course of the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 24.6mm per hour and a total rainfall of 47.89mm.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning activity recorded in a single day is 23, which occurred on 07 September 2025 (since our records began on 11 April 2025).

storm.status: Currently no stormy weather.  The most recent storm happened on 21 December 2025 and it lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not an active cold snap.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C) on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is currently no heatwaves.  This year there have been 6 heatwavess, usually there are 1.9 heatwavess per year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was on 18 July 2025 and it has only happened 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (193°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: A wind gust of 88.9 mph, places it as the 4th largest ever noted.  The record stands at 93.7 on 13 October 2012.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The highest wind speed ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: A significant windy period is on-going and is ranked 7th for length since 23 September 2024.  So far, it has lasted 3 consecutive days beginning 23 December 2025.  The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 2.2°C), inside temperature (down 2.0°C), and outside temperature (down 1.9°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 165 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in outside temperature (up 4.9°C), cloud levels (down 420 m), inside temperature (up 3.3°C), and outside humidity (up 8%) when compared to today.

pressure.description:
Today’s average pressure is 0.6 mbar higher than yesterday, 16.6 mbar higher than the past week, 20.2 mbar higher than the past month.

The pressures yesterday fell between 1019 mbar minimum and 1029 mbar maximum, with an average of 1026.0 mbar; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 1009.4 mbar and 1005.8 mbar.

summary.summary: Inside; temperature should remain cool. Outside; icy, freezing temperatures. Also, gentle light winds are expected. Possible light showers, clouds will occasionally block sunlight, and it’s dim outside. Stable weather patterns due to high pressure.

washing.phrase: It is nighttime; if you have washing out then bring it in as it will probably get damper overnight.
